entry.title: 5G NR The Next Generation Wireless Access Technology
entry.content:
5G NR

  • FDD 频分 TDD 时分

    FDD: 基站给 ue 发送信息用 f1 的频率，ue 给基站发送信息使用 f2 的频率.

    TDD: 基站和 ue 之间通信使用相同的频率，但在不同的时间段上进行切换（基站发送给 ue 切换到 ue 发送给基站）.

  • Time-Domain Structure

    Transmission 被分为长度为 10 ms 的 frame，每一个 frame 被分为长度为 1 ms 的 subframe，每个 subframe 由包含 14 个 OFDM symbols 的 slot 组成.

    每个 frame 有一个唯一的 System Frame Number (SFN)，SFN 的周期为 1024，SFN 会在1024 个 frame，或者说是 10.24 s 后出现.


  • Physical-Layer Control Signaling

    • Downlink

      Physical Layer - L1

      Mac Layer - L2

      • Downlink 在干什么
        1. Downlink scheduling assignments (UE 能够正常接受，解调和解码 DL-SCH 所需要的信息)
        2. Uplink scheduling grants (通知 UE 关于 UL-SCH 的传输资源和传输格式)
        3. Special purpose (symbols used for uplink and downlink in a set of slots, preemption indication, and power control)

      NR 中，只有一个 signal control channel type - Physical downlink control channel (PDCCH).

      • Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH)


      payload - Downlink Control Information (DCI)

      24-bit CRC is attached to detect transmission errors.

      RNTI modifies the CRC by using a scrambling operation.

      DCI 通过解码 CRC 得出，并不会 explicitly 传输，从而减少 bits 的使用.

      The RNTI does NOT necessarily have to be the identity of the device, the C-RNTI, but can also be different types of group or common RNTIs, for example to indicate paging or a random-access response.

      • Control-channel Elements (CCEs) - 当系统给 UE 发送控制信息的时候，需要使用 PDCCH。而 CCEs 是 PDCCH 使用的资源的基本单位，它指定了哪些资源用于 PDCCH 传输.
      • Resource-element groups (REGs) - 一组 Resource element. RE 可以看作是二维时间-频率网格上的一个点.

      PDCCH的资源是按REGs来分配的，而不是单独的RE.

      • CORESET

        CORESET is a set of physical resources(i.e, a specific area on NR Downlink Resource Grid) and a set of parameters that is used to carry PDCCH/DCI.

        和 LTE 不同的是，NR CORESET 是在频域中的一个特定的区域.


        A CORESET can accommodate multiples of Aggregation Level. An Aggregation Level is made up of N CCE. A CCE is made up of 6 REGs. A REG is made up of 1 RB and 1 OFDM Symbol.


      • RE, REG, REG Bundles, CCE, Aggregation Level, CORESET 的组成


        RE: the smallest unit of the resource grid.

        REG: it is made up of 12 resource elements in frequency domain and one OFDM symbol in time domain.

        REG Bundles: multiple REGs. The size of bundles is L which is determined by the RRC parameter reg-bundle-size .

        CCE: multiple REGs.

        Aggregation Level: indicates how many CCEs are allocated.

        CORESET: CORESET is defined from a device perspective and only indicates where a device may receive PDCCH transmissions. It does not say anything on whether the gNB actually transmits a PDCCH or not.

  • K0, K1, K2的含义

    5G-NR K0, K1 & K2

Random Access Channel (RACH)

  • Aim: For establishing Uplink Synchronization between a device and a network (gNB).
  • 手机/电脑第一次连接网络的时候（或者很长时间之后再次连接网络的时候），需要 RACH 去请求访问网络.

RACH Overall Procedure


Contention Based RACH Procedure

在 UE 以一种特定的模式发送 Preamble，被称为 Signature. UE 随机选择一个 Signature，因此会出现冲突，即多个 UE 选择了完全相同的 Signature.

会通过 “Contention Resolution” step 来解决冲突.

  • 步骤：
    • UE → gNB: Preamble (RA-RNTI, L2/L3 消息大小)
    • UE ← gNB: TC-RNTI
    • UE → gNB: L2/L3 msg

如果多个 UE 选择了完全相同的 Preamble，gNB 只会解析其中一个的信息，其余的 UE 就会知道发生了冲突，从而回到第一步重新进行连接的步骤.

成功的 UE 会收到一个唯一的 C-RNTI.

Non-Contention Based RACH Procedure

entry.title: The Game of Go - 围棋AI定式 - 星位小飞
entry.content:
Foreword

It’s been a decade since I last played Go, and my heart heavies with nostalgia for the days when I was just a young primary school student discovering the game for the first time. I competed in tournaments and relished in the thrill of victory, reaching the pinnacle of my amateur career with a nidan ranking. Life got busy and my academic schedule took over, but the love for Go never faded.

In this decade, the world of Go has changed dramatically. The rise of AI has brought new strategies and techniques that have revolutionized the game. Some josekis taught by my teachers from ten years ago are now obsolete and have been rendered “useless” in the face of AI’s dominance.

But I am determined to reignite my passion for Go. To rediscover the beauty and depth of this ancient game. I will document my journey as I learn Go “all over again”, overcoming the challenges and embracing the changes. This series of blogs will be my personal testament to my love for Go.

Due to the limitations of my language proficiency, I am unable to accurately convey the technical terminology used in the game of Go in English. As a result, the relevant blog posts will be presented in Chinese.

如何配置围棋 AI

  • 棋盘：开源软件 Sabaki，设计简约美观，支持 GTP 协议，可以导入 AI

  • AI：这里有可供选择的引擎列表，目前选择了主流的 KataGo

导入 AI 后，可以载入引擎，与 AI 对弈。


载入引擎后的 Sabaki

星位小飞

星位小飞是布局阶段最常见的下法之一，后续下法从若干年前的小飞进角演变为了如今流行的托。

被淘汰的小飞进角


白棋小飞挂角后，小飞进角

从结果看，白 6 下完后，是一个两分的局面。但其中白 4 的小飞对于黑棋来说并不是先手。即使黑棋脱先后被白棋小尖进角（如下图所示），黑棋只需拆边即可，白棋对黑棋并没有什么好办法。


白 1 小尖进角，黑 2 拆边

因此白棋小飞进角过缓，也就此退出了历史舞台。

白棋 - 托

白 4 托相较于二路的飞更为高效，如果黑棋脱先被白棋长进角，黑棋再拆边时，白棋对黑棋更具压迫。


白 4 托

因此黑棋此时并不能脱先，需要板住白棋。白棋随后连板。

  • 黑 7 粘是可行的下法，白棋虎补住断点，双方定型。

黑 7 粘
  • 黑 7 立，此时黑棋头较软，白棋虎，黑棋打吃，白棋粘（开局阶段并没有什么劫材，此时白棋不应该做劫）。随后黑打吃，白棋拆边。

黑 7 立
  • 黑 7 虎，白棋同样选择虎住断点，此时黑棋长。如果黑棋不长选择脱先，被白棋虎出来，白棋棋型饱满，对黑棋很有压迫力。

黑 7 虎

  • 黑 7 打吃为主流下法。白棋只能粘住，随后黑棋长。白 10 断，之后会形成一个两分的变化。黑棋拐，白棋双打吃，黑棋长，白棋需要在角上补活。黑 15 打吃。

    此时白棋取实地，黑棋得外势。但黑棋外势存在弱点，即白棋粘。


    黑 7 打吃

    • 当白棋粘住后，黑棋可以选择在 A 或 B 处与白棋展开作战。


      白棋粘住，抓住黑棋外势弱点

entry.title: TinyKV - A KV Storage System With The Raft
entry.content:
The TinyKV course builds a key-value storage system with the Raft consensus algorithm. It is inspired by MIT 6.824 and TiKV Project.

The original talent-plan/tinykv repo is here.

My implementation can be found here.

Project 1 Standalone KV

Project 1 has two steps:

  1. Implement a standalone storage engine.
  2. Implement raw key/value service handlers.

The Column Family (CF) in project 1 is quite simple. It uses _ to combine cf and key. In the following example, this method converts kv db to multi columns.

KeyAgeClassID
Tom18Test112233
KeyValue
Age_Tom18
Class_TomTest
ID_Tom112233

Implement a standalone storage engine

Involved file: ./kv/storage/standalone_storage/standalone_storage.go

StandAloneStorage just contains one element baderDB with the type *badger.DB which provides functions to interact with Badger.

Since StandAloneStorage is an implementation of the interface Storage, it needs to implement four functions of it.

  1. Start()

    We have already created StandAloneStorage instance in NewStandAloneStorage() function, therefore we do nothing in Start().

  2. Stop()

    Stop() is used to close the badgerDB.

  3. Write()

    Before implementing Write(), let’s have a look at the two things.

    1. The second parameter []storage.Modify.
    2. engine_util package.

    • Modify is a single modification to TinyKV’s underlying storage. There are two operations Put and Delete.

      We can determine whether it is Put or Delete to perform the corresponding operations.

    • engine_util helps us to read, write and iterate.

      Here is the list of functions I used in Project 1:

      • KeyWithCF
      • GetCFFromTxn
      • NewCFIterator

  4. Reader()

    Reader() returns StorageReader which is an interface with GetCF(), IterCF() and Close().

    We create a structure StandAloneReader with element *badger.Txn as shown below.

    GetCF(), IterCF() and Close() are shown below.

Test Results of Project 1

To be continued…

entry.title: ETT - A Golang Web Framework
entry.content:
ETT means one in Swedish. This is also the first web framework I learned to implement.

Implementation of ETT can be found here.

ETT is an implementation based on the well-known framework Gin.

ETT contains the following components.

  1. Context: encapsulate Request and Respons.
  2. Trie Tree: achieve dynamic routing.
  3. Route Group Control: achieve group control.
  4. Middleware: support middleware.

How To Use ETT

Example.go

  • How to use group

  • How to use middleware

  • Panic recovery

Details of ETT

ServeHTTP Interface

ServeHTTP is an interface. When using http package, it requires an address to listen on and a handler to handle requests.

All HTTP requests will be handled if we implements the ServeHTTP interface and pass corresponding instance of handler to ListenAndServe function.

If no handler found, it will use DefaultServeMux, otherwise, our implementation of handler will be called.

Context

Context encapsulates Request and Response as well as supports JSON and HTML type. The reason behind Context is that in some scenarios it will become repetitive and cumbersome to create Header and Body of HTTP responses.

Context is created with each request and destroyed at the end of the request. All information strongly related to the current requests is carried by Context. This design keeps the scalability and complexity internal and simplifies the interface externally.

Context is like Doraemon’s pocket, you can find anything you want in it.

Dynamic Route

In addition to fixed routes, dynamic routes are widely used, for example, /hello/:name can match /hello/world, /hello/Tim, etc.

Trie is an advanced data structure that can be used to achieve dynamic route.

For example, we define the following rules.

  • /:lang/doc, /:lang/tutorial, /:lang/intro

  • /about

  • /tag/data, /tag/string

We can use Trie to represent them as shown in the following figure.

When we need to find an endpoint, start from the root node, and if none of the nodes at an intermediate level satisfy the condition, then it means there is no matched route.

Implementation of Trie

Nodes are designed as shown below.

pattern is the route to be matched, e.g. /index/:lang.

part means the part of pattern, e.g. /:lang.

children stores the child nodes.

isWild denotes if it needs wildcard match.

  • Registration of route

    When developing, routes need to be registered and later will be matched and find corresponding handlers. Therefore, Trie supports insert and search functions.

    • Insert

      Recursively find nodes at each level of the tree, and if there is no match for the current part, create a new node.

    • Search

      Recursively queries the nodes at each level, with exit rules as, match to *, match to nil, or match to the len(parts) level node.

  • Router

    Router is used to add and get routes.

    roots stores the root node of Trie, and use handler to store the HandlerFunc of each request method.

    The return value of getRoute is a map. For example, /p/go/doc matches /p/:lang/doc, so the return value is {lang: "go"}.

Route Group Control

In real developing scenarios, a set of routes usually need similar processing. For example, routes starting with /admin need authentication, etc. In our implementation, we support group control distinguished by prefixes and also nesting of groups.

Middleware

ETT framework needs to have a function that allows users to define their own functionality to be embedded in the framework as if the functionality is natively supported by the framework.

The definition of ETT’s middleware is consistent with the Handler for route mapping, where the input for processing is Context. The insertion point is that after the framework receives the request to initialise the Context, it allows the user to use their own defined middleware to do some additional processing, such as logging, and secondary processing of the Context. In addition by calling the (*Context).Next() function, the middleware can wait for the end of the user-defined Handler processing, and then do some additional operations, such as calculating the time used for this processing and so on. That is, ETT’s middleware supports the user to do some additional operations before and after the request is processed. Meanwhile, it supports setting up multiple middleware and calling them in sequence.

entry.title: Hello World
entry.content:
Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub.

Quick Start

Create a new post

More info: Writing

Run server

More info: Server

Generate static files

More info: Generating

Deploy to remote sites

More info: Deployment
